title: Volunteers Needed — May One Match Day
summary: May One Match Day is just two weeks away, and we need your help on the phones! Volunteer for the MOM Day phone bank, and enjoy coffee, snacks, and fun during our one-day fundraising extravaganza. Click the “Volunteer Now” button to sign up! Thank you so much to Water Wagon, Ruby Marine, Georgene Nel...

title: Stakeholder Survey – FY27 Budget Development
summary: EXTENDED TO MAY 16, 2025 The Trust has started working on the FY27 budget planning process. As a vital Trust stakeholder and partner, please complete our Stakeholder Survey . In doing so, you will help identify key issues and themes that are important for us to consider in our budget development pro...

title: 2025 Photo Contest Winners
summary: 2025 Photo Contest Winners: BSNC is pleased to announce the winners of the 2025 Annual Photo Contest! BSNC thanks all the entrants for the wonderful photos they submitted. These photos showcase the beauty and strength of Our People, our culture and our region. BSNC added “Our Youth, Our Future” this...

title: Your 2025 Spirit of KUAC Award winner is Leone!
summary: 2025’s Spirit of KUAC volunteer award goes to Leone! As an on-air host and regular volunteer, Leone both connects our community via her program Postcards, and contributes to our success by helping out with our live fundraising events. Leone often comes to help in the FM booth on short notice, and tr...

title: AST DUI, MICS 5: Fairbanks [AK25035251]
summary: On April 15 2025 at approximately 1424 hours, Alaska State Troopers contacted Peter Johnson (63M) who was parked in the Alaska Club parking lot. An open can of twisted tea was observed in the center cupholder. Following an investigation, Peter was arrested on suspicion of DUI (Drugs). Peter was rema...
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Warrant Arrest: Tanacross [AK25023405]
summary: On April 14, 2025 at about 1:29 PM, Alaska State Troopers contacted Kyle Allen, age 28 of Tanacross, at a residence in Tanacross. Allen was subsequently arrested on his outstanding misdemeanor arrest warrant issued for Failure to Appear at an Adjudication Hearing. Allen was remanded to Fairbanks Correctional Center where he was held on a $2500 warrant with a 10% cash posting requirement.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: his outstanding misdemeanor arrest warrant issued for Failure to Appear at

title: AST Vehicle Pursuit: Wasilla [AK25035267]
summary: On 04/15/2025 at approximately 1524 hours, Alaska State Troopers responded to a residence on Johnson Road in Wasilla for a possible Burglary in Progress. Upon arrival, the suspect, later identified as Michael Hegener age 39 of Big Lake, fled the property in his vehicle. Troopers pursued the vehicle into the Big Lake area and after successfully deploying a spike strip and Pursuit Intervention Technique (PIT), Hegener fled on foot. He was quickly arrested after being Tazed. Hegener was arrested for Failure to Stop of Direction of Peace Officer, Criminal Trespass I, Criminal Mischief, Reckless Driving, and Probation Violation. He was remanded to the Mat-Su Pretrial Facility and held without bail. Hegener was also arrested for a DV Assault from earlier in the day (see AK25035144).
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

charges: Failure to Stop of Direction of Peace Officer

title: Thinking with Ice: Sailing the Northwest Passage
summary: This piece focuses on the agency of ice in the Northwest Passage. The agency of ice becomes tangible when sailing the NWP with a sailboat. The post Thinking with Ice: Sailing the Northwest Passage appeared first on The Arctic Institute - Center for Circumpolar Security Studies .
